leadText: Poison Point (Japanese: どくのトゲ Poison Thorns) is an Ability introduced in Generation III.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation III 1.1.2 Generation IV onward 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] When a Pokémon with this Ability is hit by a move that makes contact , there is a 1/3 chance that the attacking Pokémon will become poisoned . This Ability cannot poison Poison-type or Steel-type Pokémon. If a Pokémon with this Ability is hit by a multistrike move that makes contact (such as Fury Swipes ), each hit has an independent chance to activate this Ability. Generation IV onward [ edit | edit source ] The chance for Poison Point to poison is lowered to 30%. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Poison Point has no effect outside of battle.

sections.title: In battle
sections.text: In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] When a Pokémon with this Ability is hit by a move that makes contact , there is a 1/3 chance that the attacking Pokémon will become poisoned . This Ability cannot poison Poison-type or Steel-type Pokémon. If a Pokémon with this Ability is hit by a multistrike move that makes contact (such as Fury Swipes ), each hit has an independent chance to activate this Ability. Generation IV onward [ edit | edit source ] The chance for Poison Point to poison is lowered to 30%.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Poisons attackers. MD T D S Poisons an attacker who lands a direct-contact attack. B S L こうげきしてきた ポケモンを どくじょうたいに してしまう Conq. Contact with the Pokémon may poison the attacker. MD GtI May inflict the Poisoned status on the attacker who hits it with a direct attack. * When the Pokémon is hit with a direct attack like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker is sometimes afflicted with the Poisoned status condition! * S MD When the Pokémon is hit with a contact move like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker sometimes gets poisoned! MD RT DX When the Pokémon is hit with a contact move, like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker sometimes gets poisoned.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The Ability Poison Touch introduced in Generation V can be thought of as having Poison Point's inverse effect. Prior to becoming an Ability, Poison Point appeared as a TCG Attack . Seadra is the only Pokémon that may have the Poison Point Ability without being a Poison-type itself.
